Probate Code Section 850 Petition 1. To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Real And Personal Property And For Order Directing Its Conveyance And Transfer Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850; 2. For Injunctive Relief; 3. For An Accounting; And 4. For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Oct-15-2018 Continued To Feb-11-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Anthony Perez for Stephen Salazar via Court Call.
Andrew Schultz for Jordan Goldstein (Administrator)
Michael Dobrov for Joseph Salazar (Respondent)

The Court inquired whether parties had mediation. Mr. Dobrov stated his client did not want to go to mediation, estimated trial 3-5 days. Mr. Perez requested the matter be set for trial in February 2019. The Probate Code Section 850 Petition 1. to establish estates claim of ownership to real and personal property and for order directing its conveyance and transfer pursuant to probate code section 850; 2. for injunctive relief; 3. for an accounting; and 4. for damages for financial elder abuse is set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on February 11, 2019 at 9:30 a.m., (order for the same signed in open court/a copy of the order and notice of trial was provided to Counsel).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Probate Code Section 850 Petition 1. To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Real And Personal Property And For Order Directing Its Conveyance And Transfer Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850; 2. For Injunctive Relief; 3. For An Accounting; And 4. For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Jul-16-2018 Continued To Oct-15-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Anthony Perez appeared by telephone for Stephen Salazar (Petitioner).
Michael Dobrov for Joseph Salazar (Respondent).

Mr. Perez requested the Court allow one final continuance or set the matter out for hearing. Mr. Dobrov stated that he would like to do a little more discovery, that they have discussed mediation and that he will be encouraging his Client to consider mediation. The 850 Petition to Establish Claim of Ownership to Real and Personal Property, etc. is continued to October 15, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for one final status conference.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Probate Code Section 850 Petition 1. To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Real And Personal Property And For Order Directing Its Conveyance And Transfer Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850; 2. For Injunctive Relief; 3. For An Accounting; And 4. For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse
Off Calendar May-14-2018 Continued To Jul-16-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patricia Boyes for Stephen Salazar (Petitioner) via CourtCall
Michael Dobrov for Joseph Salazar (Respondent)

Mr. Dobrov stated that production of documents are over, does not think mediation is going to happen because his client doesn't want it, and his client would like to proceed with trial. Ms. Boyes stated that she is still waiting for subpoena documents and requested a 60 day continuance for Case Management Conference. The Probate Code Section 850 Petition, etc., is continued to July 16, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Probate Code Section 850 Petition 1. To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Real And Personal Property And For Order Directing Its Conveyance And Transfer Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850; 2. For Injunctive Relief; 3. For An Accounting; And 4. For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Feb-05-2018 Continued To May-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Dobrov for Joseph Salazar and specially appearing on behalf of Patricia Boyes for petitioner Stephen Salazar.

Mr. Dobrov confirmed that the administrator of the estate was notified of the petition. He stated that the parties are now engaged in discovery, and that counsel are agreeable to mediation. He requested that the matter be continued for 90 days for the completion of the outstanding discovery. The matter was continued to May 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

Probate Code Section 850 Petition 1. To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Real And Personal Property And For Order Directing Its Conveyance And Transfer Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850; 2. For Injunctive Relief; 3. For An Accounting; And 4. For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Dec-04-2017 Continued To Feb-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Anthony Perez for Stephen Salazar.
Michael Dobrov for Joseph Salazar (Respondent).
Andrew Schultz for and with Jordan Goldstein (Current Administrator).

Mr. Schultz stated that they are still in a preliminary phase, that his Client has collected all of the assets and that the Brothers are in discovery over the issue with the house. Mr. Dobrov stated that discovery is ongoing. Mr. Perez stated that there is a tentative deposition in January, that he expects to receive outstanding subpoenas from the medical providers, requested 60-days for a further status hearing, and stated that he is amenable to mediation, but he does not believe the case is postured at this time for mediation. Mr. Perez also stated that at this point he does not believe an injunction is needed and that the assets in question have been marshalled. The 850 Petition to Establish Estates Claim of Ownership to Real and Personal Property, etc. is continued to February 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Probate Code Section 850 Petition 1. To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Real And Personal Property And For Order Directing Its Conveyance And Transfer Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850; 2. For Injunctive Relief; 3. For An Accounting; And 4. For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Aug-21-2017 Continued To Dec-04-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Probate Code Section 850 Petition 1. To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Real And Personal Property And For Order Directing Its Conveyance And Transfer Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850; 2. For Injunctive Relief; 3. For An Accounting; And 4. For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Apr-24-2017 Continued To Aug-21-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Dobrov for Joseph Salazar (Petitioner/Respondent).
Andrew Schultz for and with Jordan Goldstein (Proposed Administrator).
Anthony Perez for Stephen Salazar (Petitioner).

Mr. Schultz stated that they are prepared to have Ms. Goldstein appointed. Mr. Dobrov stated that discovery needs to be conducted prior to doing mediation. Mr. Perez stated that he is trying to get a catalog and inventory of certain assets to evaluate the petition for injunction. The 850 Petition to Establish the Estates Claim on Ownership to Real and Personal Property, etc. is continued to August 21, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court granted Joseph Salazar's amended petition to appoint Jordan Goldstein as Administrator with bond set at 400,000.00 (subject to receipt and review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-28-2016 Continued To Jan-23-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Tyler Wicks for Stephen Salazar. Michael Dobrov for and with (contestant) Joseph Salazar.

The Court asked counsel if there is anything further from Stephen. Mr. Wicks stated that they will be filing an 850 petition, that there are concerns regarding the property, transfer and the Power of Attorney, that they would like a continuance to file their petition and to do discovery and that they are amenable to a professional fiduciary. Mr. Dobrov stated that his client would rather not have a neutral party, that he has Power of Attorney, that there are issues regarding the house and that they are okay with appointing a neutral party. The Court continued both Petitions for Letters of Administration to January 23, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and stated that the parties should be in agreement of a professional fiduciary by that time. Mr. Dobrov stated that his client is currently working on the estate for his parents and confirmed that the estate is now in abeyance.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
